mapping.a_element: finite-size corrections to extreme value distributions in iid and correlated systems
mapping.b_element: finite-size scaling of reunion probabilities near a third-order phase transition in non-intersecting Brownian motions

mapping.a_element: renormalization method for finite-size shape corrections
mapping.b_element: scaling limit near the critical point L_c(N) yielding Tracy-Widom distributions

mapping.a_element: exponent governing finite-size convergence in iid universality classes
mapping.b_element: critical exponent governing the third-order phase transition at L=L_c(N)

mapping.a_element: limit distribution of extreme values (e.g., Gumbel, Fréchet, Weibull)
mapping.b_element: Tracy-Widom distribution (GUE/GOE) for reunion probabilities or maximal height
non-intersecting Brownian motions with boundary constraints (periodic, absorbing, reflecting)

brief_justification: Both papers study finite-size scaling corrections to universal limit distributions: Paper A focuses on extreme value statistics in iid and correlated systems, while Paper B examines reunion probabilities and maximal heights in non-intersecting Brownian motions near a phase transition. The shared machinery is the renormalization/scaling approach to finite-size effects, with Tracy-Widom distributions (Paper B) playing the role of the universal extreme value distributions (Paper A).
